selection.status_reason: Live thesis monitor refreshed the active weekly pick.

reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is 6.7% and 20-day momentum is 17.0%.
Trend quality is supported by 80% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 8.4% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 1.7%, downside volatility near 1.4%, and a recent max drawdown of 5.2%.
Volume confirmation is -10.4% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is 5.6% versus SPY over 5 days and 2.0% versus the sector ETF.
News signal is bullish with score 0.84, confidence 0.81, and 0.6 weighted articles across 3 sources.
GPT-5 article classification and company-news provider coverage rated the news flow bullish at 0.84, after adjusting for relevance, recency, source quality, and story concentration.
Evidence quality: 3 articles across 3 sources, 0.6 effective weighted articles, average relevance 0.85, average source quality 0.82, confidence 0.81.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC 0.02).
Overall model score is 0.253 with high confidence.

news_evidence.title: Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) Launches Dual-Energy Cardiac Ablation Catheter in Europe
news_evidence.llm_reason: JNJ-specific product launch (dual-energy cardiac ablation catheter) in Europe is a clear company development and positive for franchise/innovation sentiment, but likely modest near-term revenue impact and limited to regional rollout — so only a moderate short-term effect.

news_evidence.title: Is It Worth Investing in Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) Based on Wall Street's Bullish Views?
news_evidence.llm_reason: Headline-format piece that rehashes Wall Street recommendations and offers no new material catalysts or data specific to JNJ; limited short-term predictive value.
